name: Freshstatus Status Page API
what_it_does: Status page and incident communication platform by Freshworks — enabling teams to create public and private status pages, communicate incidents and maintenance windows to customers, manage service components, and collect subscriber email notifications, with REST API for automated status updates and incident management.
use_cases: Creating incident reports via Freshstatus API for automated incident communication
Updating service component status via Freshstatus API for real-time status page automation
Scheduling maintenance windows via Freshstatus API for proactive customer notification
Integrating monitoring alerts with Freshstatus API for automatic status page updates
Managing subscribers via Freshstatus API for status page notification automation
not_for: Complex incident management with on-call workflows (use Freshservice or PagerDuty)
Monitoring and alerting (Freshstatus is status page only, not monitoring tool)
Teams not in Freshworks ecosystem (Atlassian Statuspage or Instatus for non-Freshworks stacks)
best_when: A Freshworks ecosystem company (Freshdesk, Freshservice, Freshchat users) needs a customer-facing status page — where Freshstatus integrates natively with Freshworks products for unified incident communication.
avoid_when: You're not in the Freshworks ecosystem, need complex on-call incident management, or need a monitoring tool (not just status page).

auth.notes: API key via HTTP Basic auth (username is API key, password blank) or Authorization: Bearer header. API key from Freshstatus account settings. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes. No webhooks documented. REST API for full status page management. Freshworks SSO integration for team access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free forever: 1 status page, 5 monitors, unlimited incidents, 100 subscribers
pricing.paid_tiers: Pro $19/month (3 pages, 20 monitors, 1,000 subscribers); Business $49/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Indian company (Freshworks, Chennai). Part of Freshworks product suite. Freshworks is publicly traded (NASDAQ: FRSH). Free plan is very generous. Freshworks customers: Freshdesk, Freshservice, Freshchat, Freshdesk Omni. Native Freshdesk integration (incidents create tickets). Multi-language status page. Custom domain on paid plans. SSL monitoring included. Service dependencies visualization. Public and private pages. iOS/Android apps. 99%+ used by Freshworks ecosystem customers. Freshstatus launched 2019 as standalone free product then Freshworks product.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with HTTP Basic auth (API key as username). No webhooks. GDPR/SOC2. Indian company (Freshworks, NASDAQ listed). Free generous tier. Freshworks ecosystem integration. Good documentation for status page operations.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Service/component reads idempotent. Incident creation: check for existing open incidents. Incident update idempotent by incident ID. Maintenance schedule idempotent by schedule ID.
known_agent_gotchas: HTTP Basic auth: API key as username, empty password — encode correctly as base64(apikey:)
Service ID (monitor ID) required for component status updates — list services first
No webhooks — must poll API for status changes or use Freshworks native integrations
Freshworks ecosystem integration (Freshdesk, Freshservice) is primary value — less useful standalone
Free tier: 100 subscribers — upgrade required for production with many notification subscribers
Indian company (Freshworks) — US and EU data residency available; SOC2 certified

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR, SOC2 compliant. HTTP Basic auth (API key as username). No OAuth2 scope granularity. NASDAQ-listed Freshworks — enterprise security posture. US/EU data residency. No webhooks (reduces attack surface). Status page data is typically public information.
